s1.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1) located around bottom of instrument panel on driver’s seat side.
With ignition ON, check “Sensor (Main) Torque” and “Sensor (Sub) Torque” in “Data list” mode on SUZUKI scan tool.
If check result is not as specified, remove steering wheel and steering lower shaft.
With ignition ON, check “Sensor (Main) Torque” and “Sensor (Sub) Torque” in “Data list” mode on SUZUKI scan tool.
If check result is not as specified, replace torque sensor (steering column assembly).
Remove steering column hole cover and steering lower shaft.
With ignition ON, measure voltage between terminals “3” – “5” and “3” – “7” at “E11” connector (1) connected to P/S control module (2). If check result is not as specified, replace steering column.

spec.title: Torque sensor voltage between terminal “3” and “5”
spec.ptxt: Steering wheel at neutral: Approx. 2.5 V
Steering wheel turned fully to right: Approx. 3.9 V
Steering wheel turned fully to left: Approx. 1.1 V

spec.title: Torque sensor voltage between terminal “3” and “7”
spec.ptxt: Steering wheel at neutral: Approx. 2.5 V
Steering wheel turned fully to right: Approx. 1.1 V
Steering wheel turned fully to left: Approx. 3.9 V
